title: Jagannath Temple
tagline: Jagannath Temple — spiritual town of Rajasthan

overview.short: Jagannath Temple (Devnagari: जगन्नाथ मन्दिर) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu and located in Alwar, India.
overview.description: Jagannath Temple (Devnagari: जगन्नाथ मन्दिर) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu and located in Alwar, India. The temple's presiding deity is anthropomorphic form of Lord Jagannath of Puri, while other two deities are SitaramJi, the Shaligram maharaj and JankiJi, the goddess Laxmi. The temple is built several meters above the ground in old part of the city. It has awe inspiring medieval architecture and rare floral motifs adorn its walls and pillars.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Indira Gandhi Stadium (Alwar)
topPlaces.description: Indira Gandhi Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium used mostly for association football and field hockey and also for athletics in Alwar, Rajasthan, India. The ground first held a first-class match in December 1993 when Rajasthan played the Vidarbha in the 1993/94 Ranji Trophy.

topPlaces.name: Alwar
topPlaces.description: Alwar (Rajasthani Pronunciation: [əlʋəɾ]) is a city in Rajasthan and the administrative headquarters of Alwar District in the state of Rajasthan. It is located 150 km south of Delhi and 150 km north of Jaipur.

topPlaces.name: Government Museum, Alwar
topPlaces.description: Government Museum, Alwar is a museum located in the Vinay Vilas Mahal of City Palace Alwar in Rajasthan, India. The museum collection captures the cultural, historical, and military past of Rajputana.

topPlaces.name: Alwar fort
topPlaces.description: Bala Quila also known Alwar Fort is a fort in Alwar in Indian state of Rajasthan. It is situated on a hill in the Aravalli Range, above the town of Alwar.

topPlaces.name: Siliserh Lake
topPlaces.description: Siliserh Lake is an artificial lake situated eight miles southwest of Alwar. It covers an area of about 7km2 and is flanked by dense woodland and cenotaphs on its embankment.

items.note: Indira Gandhi Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium used mostly for association football and field hockey and also for athletics in Alwar, Rajasthan, India. The ground first held a first-class match in December 1993 when Rajasthan played the Vidarbha in the 1993/94 Ranji Trophy.

Alwar (Rajasthani Pronunciation: [əlʋəɾ]) is a city in Rajasthan and the administrative headquarters of Alwar District in the state of Rajasthan. It is located 150 km south of Delhi and 150 km north of Jaipur.

Government Museum, Alwar is a museum located in the Vinay Vilas Mahal of City Palace Alwar in Rajasthan, India. The museum collection captures the cultural, historical, and military past of Rajputana.
Bala Quila also known Alwar Fort is a fort in Alwar in Indian state of Rajasthan. It is situated on a hill in the Aravalli Range, above the town of Alwar.
Siliserh Lake is an artificial lake situated eight miles southwest of Alwar. It covers an area of about 7km2 and is flanked by dense woodland and cenotaphs on its embankment.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jagannath Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jagannath Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jagannath Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jaipur International Airport (111 km) and the nearest railway station is Jaipur Junction (105 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jagannath Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jagannath Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jagannath Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Jagannath Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jagannath Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Indira Gandhi Stadium (Alwar), Alwar, Government Museum, Alwar, Alwar fort, Siliserh Lake.
